prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for formaldehyde (H₂C=O) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Cation: charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion: charge −1, multiplicity 2. Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). Geometry: user-supplied .xyz, single-point (no relaxation). All SCF states converged; integrity.trustworthy = true. No warnings reported.

Fukui indices per atom:
  Atom 1 (O):  f+ = 0.46814, f- = 0.46742, f0 = 0.46778, Δf = +0.00071
  Atom 2 (C):  f+ = 0.70839, f- = 0.11244, f0 = 0.41042, Δf = +0.59595
  Atom 3 (H):  f+ = −0.08827, f- = 0.21008, f0 = 0.06090, Δf = −0.29835
  Atom 4 (H):  f+ = −0.08826, f- = 0.21006, f0 = 0.06090, Δf = −0.29832

Most electrophilic site: C (atom 2), f+ = 0.70839 (headline value).
Most nucleophilic site: O (atom 1), f- = 0.46742.

Charge conservation: Σf+ = 1.0000, Σf- = 1.0000.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz
